claim: Nine Syriac pharmaceutical phrase-structure templates (TREATMENT, RECIPE_ACTION, etc.) match 109 decoded passages across all 225 folios, clustered on pharma and biological pages (f75v, f84r, f102r2, f102v2, f107r).
evidence_for: 109 matches across 225 folios is not random
Templates derived from independent primary sources (Bhayro, Budge, Müller-Kessler)
evidence_against: 109 matches / 225 folios ≈ 0.48/folio — is this above chance?
Template flexibility not specified; could be over-permissive
Brady has not published null-distribution comparison for templates
test: Replicate template matching with same 9 templates against shuffled-word baseline; compute z-score
test_threshold: z>=3.0 vs shuffle: confirmed. z<2.0: refuted.
tests_remaining: Replicate template matching with same 9 templates against shuffled-word baseline; compute z-score
reasoning: Strong test — if templates fire on shuffled corpus, whole grammar claim collapses.
source: Brady Defibaugh (April 2026), A Syriac Pharmaceutical Dispensatory Encoded in the Voynich Manuscript (DANI pipeline v31)
